aboutBullets: Just a lazy bum, now hobbyist cardmaker. Mostly into Hoyoverse games and Anime stuff.
Let there be em dashes (yes, they are AI-written with tremendous human oversight, in case it wasn't obvious)
Knows some things (not a TOTAL dunce)
Been around in the 'scene' since mid-2024, starting with pre-lobotomy CharacterAI. Yeah, **that** C.Ai.
The creator of [Discord Pals](https://github.com/TheLonelyDevil9/discord-pals) (with heavy inspiration and lots of feedback!)

aboutBullets.text: Also a contributor in the following projects I'm very passionate about currently:
children: [SillyBunny by Platerblitz](https://github.com/platberlitz/SillyBunny), a super-fast and customized fork of [SillyTavern, the frontend that started it all.](https://github.com/SillyTavern/SillyTavern)
[Marinara Engine by Marinara Spaghetti & the Pasta Devs](https://github.com/Pasta-Devs/Marinara-Engine), the next evolution in roleplaying as a branch-out from typical UIs.

topCard.jumpCopy: Only here for the cards? Use the button below or the floating shortcut on the bottom right.

philosophy.title: Cardmaking Philosophy
paragraphs: I typically collect 20-50k+ tokens of mostly canonical (unless stated otherwise) character data, to consume and condense into every single card I release. Expect them to be foot-focused, large, fleshed-out, and hopefully as lore-accurate as possible (at least as of the time the card is released).
Even OCs are fleshed out similarly, to have a detailed personality and their own quirks, then condensed.
This is all done with a massive amount of heavy lifting done by AI.
whyBullets: I was sick of cards with no substance or wrong information, and wanted cards anyone could pick up to use as a baseline, to modify to their liking, or use as-is.

~~I'm also allergic to lorebooks.~~
Wow, I just needed some motivation huh, fine. FINE. Condensed cards incoming with next round of audits.

Heavily inspired by [SleepDeprived03's work](https://discord.gg/Nbv9pQ88Xb) (search "Sleep" in the NSFW cards channel to find his two main threads - rest in peace, you legend), as well as [Bronya Rand's work](https://bronya-rand.github.io/reimagined-couscous/). Thank you for your hard work.

presetRecommendations.label: Geechan's Universal Roleplay Prompt
presetRecommendations.description:
With the version 5.2 coming up, I can confidently say it's the easiest, most excellent preset I have the pleasure of using. Highly recommended.
Has baked-in variants. Token efficient. Plug, play, forget (literally zero changes necessary... scary).
It adapts to your style without injecting its own, and however you like it is how your prose will flow.
Has provisions for reducing token usage in reasoning as well, for the particularly *scholarly* models like Kimi 2.6.

modelRecommendations.title: Model Recommendations

bullets.text: My go-to: Opus 4.6 Max for creative tasks because 4.7 is all kinds of fucked.
children: GPT-5.5 xHigh for anything execution-related. ANYTHING. Words cannot describe how good GPT 5.5 is at execution, and how badly Opus can fuck your shit up. Anthropic fucking up recently - further reading: https://clawd.rip
I used to have a saying... Opus couldn't be beat.

bullets.text: How do I afford these top tier LLMs without selling a kidney a day? I use [LinkAPI.](https://linkapi.ai/)
children: Trustworthy, great service, comms, and pricing. Works out of the box. I pay for the service too. Professional shilling.
**Update: As of 21st April 2026, ClaudeCheap and ClaudeCodeCheap groups are incredible again, with the lowest pricing to boot (think half a dollar in & $2.80 out/MToks).**

GPT 5.5 xHigh for peanuts? You got it. 15 cents in, 85 out/MToks. We're back to incredible savings.
I swear I get to shill this service for all the good reasons.

otherRecs: [Openrouter trendlists](https://openrouter.ai/rankings) for the day/week are good indicators as well.
Also, worth checking [Marinara's Recommendations in the respective tab](https://spicymarinara.github.io/) - frequently updated, includes model revisits. (that almost nobody else does)

requests.title: Requests
requests.subtext: No guarantees though, only made (at all) OR prioritized, if I like the character.

items.text: I can't believe I have to say this, but if you really nitpick measurement units as part of a global audience (cm/UK foot sizing/etc) you're going to be laughed at.
If you come to me saying the card is giving you response issues with remembering things, I'm going to laugh at you. Again. Join the discord and educate yourself, or just read Sukino's guide.
Card not to your expectations? Fork it. Zero qualms with that.
LinkAPI is partnered with Marinara's Kitchen, where I'm a member of the staff.
items.childCopy:
Most of the work I've done here on chub was only possible thanks to the service.
I am definitely going to say I'm biased towards the service, and for good reason.
Anyone else remember MegaLLM and other famous proxies?

items.text: Why, yes, I like feet... How could you tell?
